narration: Declare an integer variable named size and initialize it to zero.
Print a prompt asking the user to enter the size of the array.
Read the user input into the size variable using cin.
Declare an integer array named myArray with the size specified by the user.
Print a prompt asking the user to enter the specified number of integers in any order.
Create a for loop that iterates from 0 to size minus 1.
Inside the loop, read each integer input into the corresponding index of myArray.
Print the text 'Before sorting' to the console.
Create a for loop to iterate through the array and print each element of myArray separated by a space.
Print a newline character to move to the next line.
Call the quicksort function, passing myArray, 0 as the start index, and size minus 1 as the end index.
Print the text 'After sorting' to the console.
Create a for loop to iterate through the array and print each sorted element of myArray.
Define the quicksort function with parameters: integer array arr, integer start index s, and integer end index e.
Add a condition to check if the start index s is less than the end index e.
Inside the condition, declare an integer variable p to store the partition index.
Call the partition function with arr, s, and e, and assign the returned value to p.
Recursively call quicksort for the left partition, passing arr, s, and p minus 1.
Recursively call quicksort for the right partition, passing arr, p plus 1, and e.
Define the partition function with parameters: integer array arr, integer start index s, and integer end index e.
